summary.main_failure_mode: Satellite says Cerberus v0.5 is active, but active hooks and paths still reference v0.3/V0.02 or a broken .protocol-core.

items.evidence: Root listing shows .protocol-core -> D:\AI\Cerberus\rules; git status warns it cannot open .protocol-core.
items.risk: Auditors and hooks can resolve to a missing or wrong protocol root.
items.decision: Move or remove from live tree; do not preserve as active control.

items.evidence: Header: Coder Cerberus v0.3; invokes setup_validate.py and protocol_cli.py through .protocol-core.
items.risk: Commit gate may fail through broken protocol path or validate against obsolete semantics.
items.decision: Archive hook content as reference, then replace with no hook or a current minimal adapter after contract.

items.evidence: Header: Coder Cerberus V0.02; exits 0 when no upstream; bypasses checks when .protocol-core exists.
items.risk: Push gate can silently permit pushes without current Cerberus validation.
items.decision: Archive hook content and remove as active enforcement until current adapter is designed.

items.evidence: Claims v0.5, 'Hooks v0.5 funcionales', and binding to D:\AI\Cerberus\rules while live hooks show v0.3/V0.02 and .protocol-core is broken.
items.risk: False-positive operational state: documentation says protected while mechanisms are stale.
items.decision: Archive or regenerate after Fase 1 contract; do not trust as current handoff.

items.evidence: Header: CoderCerberus V0.02; opt-in maintenance only if CERBERUS_POSTCOMMIT_AUTOMATION=1.
items.risk: Low immediate risk, but still stale and outside tracked code.
items.decision: Archive with the other hooks during purge.

items.risk: Agents may keep invoking obsolete audit entrypoints.
items.decision: Move to deprecated or regenerate only after current adapter decision.

items.risk: Legacy audit naming remains in live project code.
items.decision: Review in Fase 1/2; purge if not part of actual product contract.

items.evidence: Tests assert Cerberus files/hooks/auditor presence rather than project user behavior.
items.risk: Testing suite may be dominated by control-plane checks instead of product evidence.
items.decision: Classify during Fase 5 as possible theater unless tied to an explicit repo contract.

items.risk: Dirty worktree and noisy audit state.
items.decision: Remove cache during purge; no reference value.
